briefdescription.para: Interface is admin up.
Interface is pointopoint.
Interface is in promiscuous mode.
Do not start the interface immediately after initialization.

detaileddescription.para: This requires that either the device driver or some other entity will need to manually take the interface up when needed. For example for Ethernet this will happen when the driver calls the net_eth_carrier_on() function.

briefdescription.para: Power management specific: interface is being suspended.
Flag defines if received multicasts of other interface are forwarded on this interface.

detaileddescription.para: This activates multicast routing / forwarding for this interface.

briefdescription.para: Interface supports IPv4.
Interface supports IPv6.
Interface up and running (ready to receive and transmit).
Driver signals L1 is up.
Driver signals dormant.
IPv6 Neighbor Discovery disabled.
IPv6 Multicast Listener Discovery disabled.
Mutex locking on TX data path disabled on the interface.
Network interface flags.
Initial (unknown) value.
Hardware missing.
Interface is down.
Lower layer interface is down.
Training mode.
Waiting external action.
Interface is up.
Network interface operational status (RFC 2863).
Interface supports IP version 4 header checksum calculation.
Interface supports checksum calculation for TCP payload in IPv4.
Interface supports checksum calculation for UDP payload in IPv4.
Interface supports checksum calculation for ICMP4 payload in IPv4.
Interface supports IP version 6 header checksum calculation.
Interface supports checksum calculation for TCP payload in IPv6.
Interface supports checksum calculation for UDP payload in IPv6.
Interface supports checksum calculation for ICMP6 payload in IPv6.
Type of checksum for which support in the interface will be queried.
A function prototype to create an offloaded socket.

detaileddescription.para: The prototype is compatible with socket() function.

briefdescription.para: Callback used while iterating over network interface IP addresses.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the network interface the address belongs to
A valid pointer to user data or NULL

briefdescription.para: Callback used while iterating over network interface multicast IP addresses.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the network interface the address belongs to
Pointer to current multicast IP address
A valid pointer to user data or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: )(struct net_if *iface, const struct net_addr *addr, bool is_joined)
memberdef.name: net_if_mcast_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Define a callback that is called whenever a IPv6 or IPv4 multicast address group is joined or left.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a struct net_if to which the multicast address is attached.
True if the multicast group is joined, false if group is left.

briefdescription.para: Define callback that is called after a network packet has been sent.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a struct net_if to which the net_pkt was sent to.
Link layer address of the destination where the network packet was sent.

briefdescription.para: Callback used while iterating over network interfaces.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to user data or NULL

briefdescription.para: Define callback that is called after a network packet has been timestamped.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a struct net_pkt which has been timestamped after being sent.

briefdescription.para: Set a value in network interface flags.
Test and set a value in network interface flags.

simplesect.para: true if the bit was set, false if it wasn't.

briefdescription.para: Clear a value in network interface flags.
Test and clear a value in network interface flags.

simplesect.para: true if the bit was set, false if it wasn't.

briefdescription.para: Check if a value in network interface flags is set.

simplesect.para: True if the value is set, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Set an operational state on an interface.

simplesect.para: The new operational state of an interface

briefdescription.para: Get an operational state of an interface.

simplesect.para: Operational state of an interface

briefdescription.para: Get an operational state change time of an interface.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to store the change time. Time the operational state of an interface was last changed, in milliseconds since boot. If the interface operational state has not been changed yet, then the value is 0.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, -EINVAL if operational state change time could not be retrieved (for example if iface or change_time is NULL).

briefdescription.para: Try sending a packet through a net iface.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure
Pointer to a net packet to send

simplesect.para: verdict about the packet

briefdescription.para: Send a packet through a net iface.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to net_if_try_queue_tx with an infinite timeout
iface
Pointer to a network interface structure
pkt
Pointer to a net packet to send
verdict about the packet

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to the interface L2.

parameterdescription.para: a valid pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: a pointer to the iface L2

briefdescription.para: Input a packet through a net iface.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure
Pointer to a net packet to input

simplesect.para: verdict about the packet

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to the interface L2 private data.

parameterdescription.para: a valid pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: a pointer to the iface L2 data

briefdescription.para: Get an network interface's device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: a pointer to the device driver instance

briefdescription.para: Try enqueuing a packet to the net interface TX queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure
Pointer to a net packet to queue

briefdescription.para: Queue a packet to the net interface TX queue.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to net_if_try_queue_tx with an infinite timeout
iface
Pointer to a network interface structure
pkt
Pointer to a net packet to queue

briefdescription.para: Return the IP offload status.

simplesect.para: True if IP offloading is active, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Return offload status of a given network interface.

simplesect.para: True if IP or socket offloading is active, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Return the IP offload plugin.

simplesect.para: NULL if there is no offload plugin defined, valid pointer otherwise

briefdescription.para: Set the IP offload plugin.
Return the socket offload status.

simplesect.para: True if socket offloading is active, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set the function to create an offloaded socket.

parameterdescription.para: A function to create an offloaded socket

briefdescription.para: Return the function to create an offloaded socket.

simplesect.para: NULL if the interface is not socket offloaded, valid pointer otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get an network interface's link address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: a pointer to the network link address

briefdescription.para: Return network configuration for this network interface.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: Pointer to configuration

briefdescription.para: Start duplicate address detection procedure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

briefdescription.para: Start neighbor discovery and send router solicitation message.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

briefdescription.para: Stop neighbor discovery.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

briefdescription.para: Provide a reachability hint for IPv6 Neighbor Discovery.

detaileddescription.para: This function is intended for upper-layer protocols to inform the IPv6 Neighbor Discovery process about an active link to a specific neighbor. By signaling a recent "forward progress" event, such as the reception of an ACK, this function can help reduce unnecessary ND traffic as per the guidelines in RFC 4861 (section 7.3).

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the network interface.
Pointer to the IPv6 address of the neighbor node.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const uint8_t *addr, uint8_t len, enum net_link_type type)
memberdef.name: net_if_set_link_addr

briefdescription.para: Set a network interface's link address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure
A pointer to a uint8_t buffer representing the address.
network bearer type of this link address

simplesect.para: 0 on success

briefdescription.para: Get an network interface's MTU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure

simplesect.para: the MTU

briefdescription.para: Set an network interface's MTU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a network interface structure
New MTU, note that we store only 16 bit mtu value.

briefdescription.para: Set the infinite status of the network interface address.
Get an interface according to link layer address.

simplesect.para: Network interface or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Find an interface from it's related device.

parameterdescription.para: A valid struct device pointer to relate with an interface

simplesect.para: a valid struct net_if pointer on success, NULL otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get network interface IP config.

simplesect.para: NULL if not found or pointer to correct config settings.

briefdescription.para: Remove a router from the system.
Set the default network interface.

parameterdescription.para: New default interface, or NULL to revert to the one set by Kconfig.

briefdescription.para: Get the default network interface.

simplesect.para: Default interface or NULL if no interfaces are configured.

briefdescription.para: Get the first network interface according to its type.

parameterdescription.para: Layer 2 type of the network interface.

simplesect.para: First network interface of a given type or NULL if no such interfaces was found.

briefdescription.para: Get the first network interface which is up.

simplesect.para: First network interface which is up or NULL if all interfaces are down.

briefdescription.para: Allocate network interface IPv6 config.

detaileddescription.para: This function will allocate new IPv6 config.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to allocated IPv6 struct is returned to caller.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Release network interface IPv6 config.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Check if this IPv6 address belongs to one of the interfaces.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Check if this IPv6 address belongs to this specific interfaces.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Check if this IPv6 address belongs to one of the interface indices.

simplesect.para: >0 if address was found in given network interface index, all other values mean address was not found

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *addr, enum net_addr_type addr_type, uint32_t vlifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_addr_add

briefdescription.para: Add a IPv6 address to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface address, NULL if cannot be added

memberdef.argsstring: (int index, const struct net_in6_addr *addr, enum net_addr_type addr_type, uint32_t vlifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_addr_add_by_index

briefdescription.para: Add a IPv6 address to an interface by index.

simplesect.para: True if ok, false if address could not be added

briefdescription.para: Update validity lifetime time of an IPv6 address.
Remove an IPv6 address from an interface.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Remove an IPv6 address from an interface by index.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Go through all IPv6 addresses on a network interface and call callback for each used address.
Add a IPv6 multicast address to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface multicast address, NULL if cannot be added

briefdescription.para: Remove an IPv6 multicast address from an interface.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Go through all IPv6 multicast addresses on a network interface and call callback for each used address.
Check if this IPv6 multicast address belongs to a specific interface or one of the interfaces.

parameterdescription.para: If *iface is null, then pointer to interface is returned, otherwise the *iface value needs to be matched.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface multicast address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Register a multicast monitor.

parameterdescription.para: Monitor handle. This is a pointer to a monitor storage structure which should be allocated by caller, but does not need to be initialized.
Network interface or NULL for all interfaces

briefdescription.para: Unregister a multicast monitor.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_addr *addr, bool is_joined)
memberdef.name: net_if_mcast_monitor

briefdescription.para: Call registered multicast monitors.

parameterdescription.para: Is this multicast address group joined (true) or not (false)

briefdescription.para: Mark a given multicast address to be joined.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface the address belongs to

briefdescription.para: Check if given multicast address is joined or not.

simplesect.para: True if address is joined, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Mark a given multicast address to be left.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface the address belongs to

briefdescription.para: Return prefix that corresponds to this IPv6 address.

simplesect.para: Pointer to prefix, NULL if not found.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *addr, uint8_t len)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_prefix_lookup

briefdescription.para: Check if this IPv6 prefix belongs to this interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to prefix, NULL if not found.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *prefix, uint8_t len, uint32_t lifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_prefix_add

briefdescription.para: Add a IPv6 prefix to an network interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to prefix, NULL if the prefix was not added.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *addr, uint8_t len)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_prefix_rm

briefdescription.para: Remove an IPv6 prefix from an interface.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Set the infinite status of the prefix.
Set the prefix lifetime timer.
Unset the prefix lifetime timer.
Check if this IPv6 address is part of the subnet of our network interface.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface. This is returned to the caller. The iface can be NULL in which case we check all the interfaces.

simplesect.para: True if address is part of our subnet, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get the IPv6 address of the given router.

simplesect.para: pointer to the IPv6 address, or NULL if none

briefdescription.para: Check if IPv6 address is one of the routers configured in the system.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if cannot be found

briefdescription.para: Find default router for this IPv6 address.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface. This can be NULL in which case we go through all the network interfaces to find a suitable router.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if cannot be found

briefdescription.para: Update validity lifetime time of a router.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *addr, bool is_default, uint16_t router_lifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_router_add

briefdescription.para: Add IPv6 router to the system.

parameterdescription.para: Is this router the default one
Lifetime of the router. 0 if the router never expire.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if could not be added

briefdescription.para: Remove IPv6 router from the system.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get IPv6 hop limit specified for a given interface.

detaileddescription.para: This is the default value but can be overridden by the user.

simplesect.para: Hop limit

briefdescription.para: Set the default IPv6 hop limit of a given interface.
Get IPv6 multicast hop limit specified for a given interface.

detaileddescription.para: This is the default value but can be overridden by the user.

simplesect.para: Hop limit

briefdescription.para: Set the default IPv6 multicast hop limit of a given interface.
Set IPv6 reachable time for a given interface.
Get IPv6 reachable timeout specified for a given interface.

simplesect.para: Reachable timeout

briefdescription.para: Calculate next reachable time value for IPv6 reachable time.

simplesect.para: Reachable time

briefdescription.para: Set IPv6 reachable time for a given interface.

detaileddescription.para: This requires that base reachable time is set for the interface.

briefdescription.para: Set IPv6 retransmit timer for a given interface.
Get IPv6 retransmit timer specified for a given interface.

simplesect.para: Retransmit timer

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv6 source address that should be used when sending network data to destination.

parameterdescription.para: Interface that was used when packet was received. If the interface is not known, then NULL can be given.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 address to use, NULL if no IPv6 address could be found.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *dst, int flags)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv6_select_src_addr_hint

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv6 source address that should be used when sending network data to destination.

detaileddescription.para: Use a hint set to the socket to select the proper address.

parameterdescription.para: Interface that was used when packet was received. If the interface is not known, then NULL can be given.
Hint from the related socket. See RFC 5014 for value details.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 address to use, NULL if no IPv6 address could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a network interface that should be used when sending IPv6 network data to destination.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface to use, NULL if no suitable interface could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a network interface that should be used when sending IPv6 network data to destination.

detaileddescription.para: Also return the source IPv6 address from that network interface.

parameterdescription.para: IPv6 source address. This can be set to NULL if the source address is not needed.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface to use, NULL if no suitable interface could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv6 link local address in a given state.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to link local IPv6 address, NULL if no proper IPv6 address could be found.

briefdescription.para: Return link local IPv6 address from the first interface that has a link local address matching give state.

parameterdescription.para: IPv6 address state (ANY, TENTATIVE, PREFERRED, DEPRECATED)

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Stop IPv6 Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) procedure if we find out that our IPv6 address is already in use.

parameterdescription.para: Interface where the DAD was running.

briefdescription.para: Return global IPv6 address from the first interface that has a global IPv6 address matching the given state.

parameterdescription.para: IPv6 address state (ANY, TENTATIVE, PREFERRED, DEPRECATED)
Caller can give an interface to check. If iface is set to NULL, then all the interfaces are checked. Pointer to interface where the IPv6 address is defined is returned to the caller.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Allocate network interface IPv4 config.

detaileddescription.para: This function will allocate new IPv4 config.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to allocated IPv4 struct is returned to caller.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Release network interface IPv4 config.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Get IPv4 time-to-live value specified for a given interface.

simplesect.para: Time-to-live

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 time-to-live value specified to a given interface.
Get IPv4 multicast time-to-live value specified for a given interface.

simplesect.para: Time-to-live

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 multicast time-to-live value specified to a given interface.
Check if this IPv4 address belongs to one of the interfaces.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface address, NULL if not found.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in_addr *addr, enum net_addr_type addr_type, uint32_t vlifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv4_addr_add

briefdescription.para: Add a IPv4 address to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface address, NULL if cannot be added

briefdescription.para: Remove a IPv4 address from an interface.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Check if this IPv4 address belongs to one of the interface indices.

simplesect.para: >0 if address was found in given network interface index, all other values mean address was not found

memberdef.argsstring: (int index, const struct net_in_addr *addr, enum net_addr_type addr_type, uint32_t vlifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv4_addr_add_by_index

briefdescription.para: Add a IPv4 address to an interface by network interface index.

simplesect.para: True if ok, false if the address could not be added

briefdescription.para: Remove a IPv4 address from an interface by interface index.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Go through all IPv4 addresses on a network interface and call callback for each used address.
Add a IPv4 multicast address to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface multicast address, NULL if cannot be added

briefdescription.para: Remove an IPv4 multicast address from an interface.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Go through all IPv4 multicast addresses on a network interface and call callback for each used address.
Check if this IPv4 multicast address belongs to a specific interface or one of the interfaces.

parameterdescription.para: If *iface is null, then pointer to interface is returned, otherwise the *iface value needs to be matched.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface multicast address, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Mark a given multicast address to be joined.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface the address belongs to

briefdescription.para: Check if given multicast address is joined or not.

simplesect.para: True if address is joined, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Mark a given multicast address to be left.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface the address belongs to

briefdescription.para: Get the IPv4 address of the given router.

simplesect.para: pointer to the IPv4 address, or NULL if none

briefdescription.para: Check if IPv4 address is one of the routers configured in the system.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if cannot be found

briefdescription.para: Find default router for this IPv4 address.

parameterdescription.para: Network interface. This can be NULL in which case we go through all the network interfaces to find a suitable router.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if cannot be found

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in_addr *addr, bool is_default, uint16_t router_lifetime)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv4_router_add

briefdescription.para: Add IPv4 router to the system.

parameterdescription.para: Is this router the default one
Lifetime of the router. 0 if the router never expire.

simplesect.para: Pointer to router information, NULL if could not be added

briefdescription.para: Remove IPv4 router from the system.

simplesect.para: True if successfully removed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv4 address belongs to local subnet.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.

simplesect.para: True if address is part of local subnet, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv4 address is a broadcast address.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.
IPv4 address, this should be in network byte order

simplesect.para: True if address is a broadcast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a network interface that should be used when sending IPv4 network data to destination.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface to use, NULL if no suitable interface could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a network interface that should be used when sending IPv4 network data to destination.

detaileddescription.para: Also return the source IPv4 address from that network interface.

parameterdescription.para: IPv4 source address. This can be set to NULL if the source address is not needed.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface to use, NULL if no suitable interface could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv4 source address that should be used when sending network data to destination.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use when sending the packet. If the interface is not known, then NULL can be given.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv4 address to use, NULL if no IPv4 address could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv4 link local address in a given state.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to link local IPv4 address, NULL if no proper IPv4 address could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get a IPv4 global address in a given state.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to link local IPv4 address, NULL if no proper IPv4 address could be found.

briefdescription.para: Get IPv4 netmask related to an address of an interface.

simplesect.para: The netmask set on the interface related to the give address, unspecified address if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get IPv4 netmask of an interface.

simplesect.para: The netmask set on the interface, unspecified address if not found.

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 netmask for an interface.
Set IPv4 netmask for an interface index.

simplesect.para: True if netmask was added, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (int index, const struct net_in_addr *addr, const struct net_in_addr *netmask)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv4_set_netmask_by_addr_by_index

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 netmask for an interface index for a given address.

parameterdescription.para: IPv4 address related to this netmask

simplesect.para: True if netmask was added, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in_addr *addr, const struct net_in_addr *netmask)
memberdef.name: net_if_ipv4_set_netmask_by_addr

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 netmask for an interface index for a given address.

parameterdescription.para: IPv4 address related to this netmask

simplesect.para: True if netmask was added, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get IPv4 gateway of an interface.

simplesect.para: The gateway set on the interface, unspecified address if not found.

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 gateway for an interface.
Set IPv4 gateway for an interface index.

simplesect.para: True if gateway was added, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a network interface that should be used when sending IPv6 or IPv4 network data to destination.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface to use. Note that the function will return the default network interface if the best network interface is not found.

briefdescription.para: Register a link callback.

parameterdescription.para: Caller specified handler for the callback.

briefdescription.para: Unregister a link callback.

parameterdescription.para: Caller specified handler for the callback.

briefdescription.para: Call a link callback function.
Check if received network packet checksum calculation can be avoided or not.

detaileddescription.para: For example many ethernet devices support network packet offloading in which case the IP stack does not need to calculate the checksum.

parameterdescription.para: L3 and/or L4 protocol for which to compute checksum

simplesect.para: True if checksum needs to be calculated, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if network packet checksum calculation can be avoided or not when sending the packet.

detaileddescription.para: For example many ethernet devices support network packet offloading in which case the IP stack does not need to calculate the checksum.

parameterdescription.para: L3 and/or L4 protocol for which to compute checksum

simplesect.para: True if checksum needs to be calculated, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get interface according to index.

detaileddescription.para: This is a syscall only to provide access to the object for purposes of assigning permissions.

simplesect.para: Pointer to interface or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get interface index according to pointer.

simplesect.para: Interface index

briefdescription.para: Go through all the network interfaces and call callback for each interface.
Bring interface up.

simplesect.para: 0 on success

briefdescription.para: Check if interface is up and running.

simplesect.para: True if interface is up, False if it is down.

briefdescription.para: Bring interface down.

simplesect.para: 0 on success

briefdescription.para: Check if interface was brought up by the administrator.

simplesect.para: True if interface is admin up, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Underlying network device has detected the carrier (cable connected).

detaileddescription.para: The function should be used by the respective network device driver or L2 implementation to update its state on a network interface.

briefdescription.para: Underlying network device has lost the carrier (cable disconnected).

detaileddescription.para: The function should be used by the respective network device driver or L2 implementation to update its state on a network interface.

briefdescription.para: Check if carrier is present on network device.

simplesect.para: True if carrier is present, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Mark interface as dormant.

detaileddescription.para: Dormant state indicates that the interface is not ready to pass packets yet, but is waiting for some event (for example Wi-Fi network association).
The function should be used by the respective network device driver or L2 implementation to update its state on a network interface.

briefdescription.para: Mark interface as not dormant.

detaileddescription.para: The function should be used by the respective network device driver or L2 implementation to update its state on a network interface.

briefdescription.para: Check if the interface is dormant.

simplesect.para: True if interface is dormant, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if_timestamp_cb *handle, struct net_pkt *pkt, struct net_if *iface, net_if_timestamp_callback_t cb)
memberdef.name: net_if_register_timestamp_cb

briefdescription.para: Register a timestamp callback.

parameterdescription.para: Caller specified handler for the callback.
Net packet for which the callback is registered. NULL for all packets.
Net interface for which the callback is. NULL for all interfaces.

briefdescription.para: Unregister a timestamp callback.

parameterdescription.para: Caller specified handler for the callback.

briefdescription.para: Call a timestamp callback function.
Set network interface into promiscuous mode.

detaileddescription.para: Note that not all network technologies will support this.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Set network interface into normal mode.
Check if promiscuous mode is set or not.

simplesect.para: True if interface is in promisc mode, False if interface is not in promiscuous mode.

briefdescription.para: Check if there are any pending TX network data for a given network interface.

simplesect.para: True if there are pending TX network packets for this network interface, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the network interface supports Wi-Fi.

simplesect.para: True if interface supports Wi-Fi, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get first Wi-Fi network interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get Wi-Fi network station interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get first Wi-Fi network Soft-AP interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to network interface, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get network interface name.

detaileddescription.para: If interface name support is not enabled, empty string is returned.

parameterdescription.para: Length of the user supplied buffer

simplesect.para: Length of the interface name copied to buf, -EINVAL if invalid parameters, -ERANGE if name cannot be copied to the user supplied buffer, -ENOTSUP if interface name support is disabled,

briefdescription.para: Set network interface name.

detaileddescription.para: Normally this function is not needed to call as the system will automatically assign a name to the network interface.

simplesect.para: 0 name is set correctly -ENOTSUP interface name support is disabled -EINVAL if invalid parameters are given, -ENAMETOOLONG if name is too long

briefdescription.para: Get interface index according to its name.

simplesect.para: Interface index

briefdescription.para: Forward declaration of a network interface.

detaileddescription.para: Enables to use of NET_IF_GET above the instantiation macro.

parameterdescription.para: Device ID provided to NET_IF_INIT or NET_IF_OFFLOAD_INIT

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_INIT
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_INIT(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, init_fn, pm, \ data, config, prio, api, l2, l2_ctx_type, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Create a network interface and bind it to network device.

parameterdescription.para: The name this instance of the driver exposes to the system.
Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Network L2 layer for this network interface.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_INIT(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), init_fn, pm, data, \ config, prio, api, l2, l2_ctx_type, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_INIT but taking metadata from a devicetree node.

detaileddescription.para: Create a network interface and bind it to network device.

parameterdescription.para: Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Network L2 layer for this network interface.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE.
other parameters as expected by NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE.

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_INIT_INSTANCE
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_INIT_INSTANCE(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, \ instance, init_fn, pm, data, config, \ prio, api, l2, l2_ctx_type, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Create multiple network interfaces and bind them to network device.

detaileddescription.para: If your network device needs more than one instance of a network interface, use this macro below and provide a different instance suffix each time (0, 1, 2, ... or a, b, c ... whatever works for you)

parameterdescription.para: The name this instance of the driver exposes to the system.
Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Network L2 layer for this network interface.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE_INSTANCE
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_INIT_INSTANCE(node_id, \ Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), instance, \ init_fn, pm, data, config, prio, \ api, l2, l2_ctx_type, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_OFFLOAD_INIT but taking metadata from a devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: Create multiple network interfaces and bind them to network device. If your network device needs more than one instance of a network interface, use this macro below and provide a different instance suffix each time (0, 1, 2, ... or a, b, c ... whatever works for you)

parameterdescription.para: Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Network L2 layer for this network interface.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE_INSTANCE for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE_INSTANCE.
other parameters as expected by NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE_INSTANCE.

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_OFFLOAD_INIT
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_OFFLOAD_INIT(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, \ init_fn, pm, data, config, prio, api, \ mtu)

briefdescription.para: Create a offloaded network interface and bind it to network device.

detaileddescription.para: The offloaded network interface is implemented by a device vendor HAL or similar.

parameterdescription.para: The name this instance of the driver exposes to the system.
Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

memberdef.name: NET_DEVICE_DT_OFFLOAD_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_NET_DEVICE_OFFLOAD_INIT(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), init_fn, pm, \ data, config, prio, api, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_OFFLOAD_INIT but taking metadata from a devicetree node.

detaileddescription.para: Create a offloaded network interface and bind it to network device. The offloaded network interface is implemented by a device vendor HAL or similar.

parameterdescription.para: Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

briefdescription.para: Like NET_DEVICE_DT_OFFLOAD_DEFINE for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to NET_DEVICE_DT_OFFLOAD_DEFINE.
other parameters as expected by NET_DEVICE_DT_OFFLOAD_DEFINE.

memberdef.name: NET_IFACE_COUNT
memberdef.initializer: do { \ extern struct net_if _net_if_list_start[]; \ extern struct net_if _net_if_list_end[]; \ *(_dst) = ((uintptr_t)_net_if_list_end - \ (uintptr_t)_net_if_list_start) / \ sizeof(struct net_if); \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: Count the number of network interfaces.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to location where result is written.

briefdescription.para: Network Interface abstraction layer.
